the more I learn about the end times the more I understand that we are living in them. I have talked to several people including Toopie and Kimberly briefly, about what I believe the end times reveal in present day. What I'm finding though is the more that I dig the more that I realize that there's other puzzle pieces that need to be addressed. That's why I'm really enjoying doing the studies because it makes me look at present day events as well as historical events in the Bible! It's been a lot of fun
notes.n-intro: My overall goal truly is to ensure that I understand many different facets of the Bible and how they tie together. It started out as a learning of the end times, and the prophecies thereof, and has evolved now into the way that I can begin to sermons or thoughts or editorials together. I want to understand more how they relate to one another period.

notes.n-t2m1:
в”Ђв”Ђ April 4, 2026 at 11:18 AM в”Ђв”Ђ
Today I worked through Module 1 of the Biblical Calendar study and came away with some things I hadn't considered before.
I started by reading Genesis 1:14 in both the NASB and The Message. What struck me is that God literally commanded the lights in the sky to mark seasons, days, and years. He built a calendar system into creation from the very beginning вЂ” this wasn't an afterthought.
I've been thinking about light and how Genesis says God created light on day one, but didn't create the sun until day four. That means the light on day one wasn't the sun. My belief is that God first created the principle and essence of light itself вЂ” the law of light вЂ” and then on day four created the sun as the physical instrument to produce and measure it. Those two ideas tie together for me.
I also realized that God rested on the seventh day and never created an eighth. That tells me the seven-day week is foundational to God's design. The Essene 364-day solar calendar is built entirely on that вЂ” 52 exact weeks, every feast and every Sabbath on the same day every year. That makes more sense to me than a lunar calendar that drifts and doesn't divide evenly into weeks.
I'm now questioning why Christianity celebrates the Sabbath on Sunday when the seventh day is clearly Saturday. That's something I need to wrestle with. I've always assumed Sunday was the Sabbath but now I'm not so sure. I understand the Resurrection connection but that doesn't necessarily mean God's design changed.
I also learned what the Torah is today вЂ” the first five books of the Bible. All the calendar commandments, feasts, and Sabbath instructions come from there. Understanding that helps me see why the calendar debate matters so much.
Two things I want to come back to:

The "nine parts" reference in the Book of Enoch вЂ” why nine and not twelve or twenty-four? I don't understand that yet.
Did high priests actually die entering the Holy of Holies on the wrong calendar day, or is that the Essenes' theological interpretation? I want to verify that before I draw any conclusions.
Approx.- 1 hour 45 minutes was actual Bible study
